title: SWC Culture Change, Module Prioritization If Underfunded, County Mosaic Alignment, and $7.8M Project Cost to Date
summary: SWC rollout driving culture change -- time sheets historically 'felt optional' with some employees not submitting for months; DocuSign delays led to paying employees after separation. SWC end of life March 2027; parallel rollout continues (timekeeping + HCM design simultaneously). TeleStaff confirmed as scheduling software repurposed for payroll; UKG Pro will properly connect scheduling with accrual management. Onondaga County also evaluating Mosaic for Kronos-to-cloud migration. Councilor Moore (experienced with Dayforce from prior NYS employment) asks about module prioritization if funding cut to 50% -- musts: demographic data, payroll, timekeeping, position management; employee self-service debated (staff argues essential for direct deposit phishing prevention, W-2 distribution, training tracking); performance management first to cut; AI chatbot module deliberately excluded from scope. Council asks total spend: $7.8M over ~5.5 years (2020-2025), roughly half SWC and half Oracle. Contractor cleanup: EY relationship ended late 2024; ERP1, Dear Alien Bait, Cherro Technologies eliminated. Active: UKG (Kronos license), Kathleen LLC (former AS400 programmer), Central City Data (former API employee doing AS400 programming).

political_signal.description: Council demanding total cost accounting after $7.8M spent and asking about module prioritization if full funding not approved

processing_notes: IPS in detected_entities is false positive (substring match from 'tips' in transcript); exclude from entity_refs
HR, Finance, API in detected_departments are all contextually correct as presenting departments
Dayforce = Ceridian HCM product (Moore's prior experience at NYS); AI chatbot = UKG module deliberately excluded
$7.8M = total project spend 2020-2025; EY = Ernst & Young consulting
Kathleen LLC and Central City Data = remaining small contractors for legacy AS400 maintenance
Segment 4 of 7 from Finance Committee Meeting, Tuesday, February 3, 2026
Potential duplicate detected: overlaps with CTX-COUNCIL-2026-00118 -- linked, not skipped
